mnemonics: Hive Formats: SERDE Your Data Right (SERDE stands for Serializer/Deserializer, a key concept in Hive for reading/writing data in different formats)
keyPoints: RCFile (Record Columnar File) stores data first by row, then by column within a row group, optimizing for both row-based queries and columnar compression.
ORCFile (Optimized Row Columnar) is an improved columnar format that provides better compression and performance than RCFile by using techniques like indexes and block-mode compression.
Parquet is a columnar storage format available across Hadoop ecosystems, highly efficient for queries that read specific columns from large datasets due to its compression and encoding schemes.
commonMistakes: Choosing the wrong file format for the workload (e.g., using text files for analytical queries instead of a columnar format like ORC or Parquet).
Not considering the schema evolution capabilities of the format, leading to errors when the data structure changes (e.g., Parquet handles this better than older formats).
